issue_title: `tensordot` not working for dtype int32 and lower when there is only 1 element in the given axis
surface_reasoning: Priority 4: labeled `actionable` + `module: linear algebra`. Open since 2022-10-01 with actionable label. Title: `tensordot` not working for dtype int32 and lower with 1-element axis — integer BLAS dispatch bug in a core linear algebra op. Domain relevance: `tensordot` with integer dtypes must dispatch to an appropriate integer BLAS routine; failure for int32 indicates a kernel dispatch gap in the integer linear algebra path.

maintainer_signals.author: zeshengzong
maintainer_signals.quote: Try following on main branch, seems already fixed and this one can be closed :D

blocking_signals: already_fixed: COLLABORATOR zeshengzong confirmed the issue is fixed on main branch (no specific PR identified, but running the repro on main produces correct output)
confidence_justification: Start: 0.50. Step 1: 'already_fixed' blocking signal present — per the precedence rule, skip all other signals and set confidence to 0.05. COLLABORATOR zeshengzong ran the exact repro script from the issue body on the main branch and confirmed it produces correct output. This is conclusive evidence the bug has been resolved. Final: 0.05.
ground_truth_delta: This finding superficially resembles positive_71774 (reproducible correctness bug in linear algebra with repro script and actionable label), but is disqualified by the hard blocker: a COLLABORATOR confirmed the issue is already fixed on the main branch. Unlike noise_76962 (CI housekeeping), this was a genuine bug, but it has since been resolved without a formal close of the issue. The issue remaining OPEN is a record-keeping oversight, not evidence of an unfixed bug.
